public_payload_preview.name: Maison Provence Restaurant
public_payload_preview.tagline: A cozy French bistro with a lush, plant-filled interior in the heart of Williams
public_payload_preview.narrative: Maison Provence presents a charming French bistro aesthetic with its plant-filled interior, blue-and-white striped awning, and warm lighting. The eclectic shelves and cozy distressed wooden chairs create an intimate, refined atmosphere in the heart of Williamsburg.

public_payload_preview.name: With Others
public_payload_preview.tagline: A warm corner bar in Williamsburg for lingering over drinks
public_payload_preview.narrative: The interior features a curved white bar counter as the focal point, flanked by warm wall sconces and ambient candles that create soft lighting throughout. Acoustic ceiling panels and a ceiling fan add to the comfortable, lived-in feel, while patrons seated at the bar and in booths suggest a lively but not overwhelming social energy.

public_payload_preview.name: Olympia
public_payload_preview.tagline: An intimate wine bar in DUMBO with warm candlelit ambiance
public_payload_preview.narrative: Olympia occupies a warm, inviting space in DUMBO with a curved wooden bar, pendant lighting, and candlelight casting a soft glow across yellow-cushioned stools and a back bar lined with bottles.

public_payload_preview.name: Anaïs
public_payload_preview.location: Brooklyn Bridge Park, New York City
public_payload_preview.tagline: A neighborhood wine bar with warm light and curated bottles
public_payload_preview.narrative: Anaïs is a neighborhood wine bar in Brooklyn with a warm interior featuring tall wine shelving, candlelit high-top tables, and large street-facing windows. Open daily from morning to late night, it offers a curated wine selection in an intimate setting.

public_payload_preview.name: Balvanera
public_payload_preview.location: Lower East Side, New York City
public_payload_preview.tagline: A Lower East Side patio with exposed brick, murals, and string lights
public_payload_preview.narrative: Balvanera anchors the Lower East Side with a sidewalk patio framed by exposed brick, a bold mural, and soft string lighting overhead. Metal bistro chairs and wooden tables create an unpretentious yet atmospheric setting for evening drinks and weekend brunches.

public_payload_preview.name: Brick Wine Bar
public_payload_preview.location: Lower East Side, New York City
public_payload_preview.tagline: A perfectly-rated wine bar in the Lower East Side with warm, candlelit evenings.
public_payload_preview.narrative: Brick Wine Bar occupies a candlelit space on Clinton Street in the Lower East Side, featuring wine bottles lining the shelves and an intimate bar setup. The cozy interior, highlighted by hanging glassware and warm lighting, creates a refined setting for evening wine experiences.

public_payload_preview.name: Ainslie Bowery
public_payload_preview.tagline: A lush Bowery bar with communal tables, brick arches, and hanging greenery
public_payload_preview.narrative: A welcoming bar on Bowery featuring long communal wooden tables beneath exposed brick arches, framed by hanging greenery and stylized artwork. Natural light filters through side windows while string lights add warmth after dark.
